flashcards.front: What is sorting in data structures?
flashcards.back: Sorting is the process of arranging data elements in a specific order (ascending or descending) to facilitate efficient searching and processing.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort in worst case?
flashcards.back: O(n²) in worst and average cases. In best case (already sorted), it is O(n) when optimized with a flag.
It selects the minimum (or maximum) element from the unsorted portion and places it at the beginning (or end) of the sorted portion.

flashcards.front: What is the key advantage of Insertion Sort?
flashcards.back: It is efficient for small data sets and nearly sorted arrays. It has O(n) best-case time complexity.

flashcards.front: What is the divide-and-conquer approach used by Merge Sort?
flashcards.back: Merge Sort divides the array into two halves, recursively sorts each half, and then merges the sorted halves back together.

flashcards.front: What is the worst-case time complexity of Quick Sort?
flashcards.back: O(n²) when the pivot is always the smallest or largest element. Average case is O(n log n).
Merge Sort is stable. Bubble Sort and Insertion Sort are also stable. Quick Sort and Selection Sort are typically unstable.
An in-place algorithm uses only a constant amount of extra memory space regardless of input size (O(1) auxiliary space).

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Merge Sort?
flashcards.back: O(n log n) in all cases (best, average, and worst). Space complexity is O(n).

flashcards.front: How does Shell Sort improve upon Insertion Sort?
flashcards.back: Shell Sort uses a gap sequence to compare elements that are far apart, allowing elements to move into position faster.
External sorting is used when data to be sorted cannot fit in memory and must be sorted using external storage (like hard drives).

flashcards.front: What is the pivot in Quick Sort?
flashcards.back: The pivot is a selected element used to partition the array into two sub-arrays: elements less than pivot and elements greater than pivot.
An adaptive sorting algorithm takes advantage of existing order in the input to perform more efficiently (e.g., Insertion Sort).

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of recursive Merge Sort?
flashcards.back: O(n) auxiliary space is required for merging. The recursion stack adds O(log n) space.

flashcards.front: What is counting sort's time complexity?
flashcards.back: O(n + k) where k is the range of input values. It is efficient for integer data within a limited range.
